name: Canadian Institutes of Health Research
description: The Canadian Institutes of Health Research (CIHR) is the Government of Canada's health research investment agency, funding health research and innovation to improve the health of Canadians.

name: Cancer Research UK
description: Cancer Research UK is the world's leading independent cancer research charity, funding scientists, doctors and nurses to help beat cancer sooner through research into prevention, diagnosis and treatment.

name: National Science Foundation
description: The National Science Foundation (NSF) is an independent US federal agency that promotes the progress of science and advances national health, prosperity, and welfare through research and education funding.

name: Academy of Finland
description: The Academy of Finland is a government agency and scientific body under the Ministry of Education and Culture, funding high-quality scientific research and advancing Finland's position as a leading research nation.

name: The Royal Society
description: The Royal Society is the UK's national academy of sciences, supporting excellence in science and encouraging the development and use of science for human benefit.

name: Wellcome Trust
description: Wellcome is a global charitable foundation that supports science to solve urgent health challenges, funding researchers and backing projects to improve health for everyone.

name: European Molecular Biology Laboratory
description: EMBL is Europe's flagship laboratory for the life sciences, conducting basic research in molecular biology and providing services and training to the scientific community.

name: Fulbright Program
description: The Fulbright Program is a prestigious international educational exchange program sponsored by the U.S. government, aimed at increasing mutual understanding between the people of the United States and other countries.

name: European Molecular Biology Organization
description: EMBO is a professional organisation of life scientists that supports talented researchers, stimulates the exchange of scientific information, and helps build a research environment where scientists can achieve their best work.

name: National Institutes of Health (NIH)
description: The National Institutes of Health (NIH) is the primary federal agency in the United States responsible for biomedical and public health research, comprising 27 institutes and centres.

name: The World Academy of Sciences
description: The World Academy of Sciences (TWAS) is an autonomous international organization that promotes sustainable prosperity through research, education, policy, and diplomacy. TWAS supports science and scientists in the developing world through fellowships, grants, and prizes.

name: Alex's Lemonade Stand Foundation for Childhood Cancer
short_name: Alex's Lemonade Stand Foundation for Childhood Cancer

name: American Academy of Family Physicians Foundation
short_name: American Academy of Family Physicians Foundation

name: American Association for Cancer Research
description: The American Association for Cancer Research (AACR) is the oldest and largest cancer research organisation in the world, accelerating progress by funding cancer research and fostering the exchange of scientific knowledge.

name: American Association for the Advancement of Science
short_name: American Association for the Advancement of Science

name: American Cancer Society
description: The American Cancer Society is a nationwide voluntary health organisation dedicated to eliminating cancer as a major health problem through research, education, advocacy, and patient services.

name: American Chemical Society
description: The American Chemical Society (ACS) is the world's largest scientific organisation of chemistry professionals, advancing the broader chemistry enterprise through research, education, and community support.

name: American Foundation for Surgery of the Hand

name: American Psychological Foundation
description: The American Psychological Foundation (APF) advances psychology as a science and profession by funding innovative grants and scholarships to support psychological research and education.

name: Australian Research Council
description: The Australian Research Council (ARC) is a government agency that manages the National Competitive Grants Program, delivering funding to support world-class research across all disciplines in Australia.

name: Burroughs Wellcome Fund
description: The Burroughs Wellcome Fund is a private charitable foundation that advances biomedical sciences by supporting research and providing career development for scientists in the early stages of their careers.

name: Pew Charitable Trusts

name: Simons Foundation

name: UK Research and Innovation
description: UK Research and Innovation (UKRI) is a non-departmental public body that directs research and innovation funding across the UK. It brings together the seven Research Councils, Innovate UK, and Research England to support science, engineering, and humanities research and innovation.

name: American Society for Biochemistry and Molecular Biology
description: ASBMB is a nonprofit scientific and educational organization that represents biochemists and molecular biologists, supporting career development through grants and awards.

name: American Society of Pediatric Hematology/Oncology
description: ASPHO is the professional home for physicians and other health professionals dedicated to the care of children with blood disorders and cancer.

name: Asia Pacific Oncology Specialists
description: Professional society dedicated to the education and professional development of oncology practitioners and researchers across the Asia Pacific region.

name: Association of Commonwealth Universities
description: The Association of Commonwealth Universities is an international organisation that connects and supports Commonwealth universities, offering grants and programmes to strengthen higher education across the Commonwealth.

name: CSIR Human Resource Development Group
aliases: Council of Scientific and Industrial Research
description: The CSIR Human Resource Development Group manages fellowships, associateships, and grants to develop scientific human resources in India.

name: European Federation of Immunological Societies
description: EFIS is the umbrella organisation of European immunological societies, promoting immunological research and supporting early career scientists through travel grants and fellowships.

name: European Scientific Working Group on Influenza
description: ESWI is a multidisciplinary group that promotes awareness of the burden of influenza and other respiratory viruses and supports early career scientists in the field.

name: International Society for Heart Research
description: International Society for Heart Research promotes research into the physiology and pathophysiology of the heart and circulation, and supports early career investigators through travel awards.

name: London Mathematical Society
description: The London Mathematical Society is the UK's learned society for mathematics, supporting mathematical research through grants, prizes, and publications.

name: PIANOFORTE Partnership
description: EU-funded research partnership advancing radiation protection research across European member states and Ukraine.

name: Plant and Animal Genome Conference
description: The Plant and Animal Genome Conference is a leading international agricultural genomics conference, offering travel grants to support attendance by students and early career researchers.

name: Rockefeller University Press
description: Publisher of peer-reviewed journals including the Journal of Experimental Medicine (JEM), Journal of Cell Biology (JCB), and Journal of General Physiology (JGP).

name: Royal Society of Chemistry
description: The Royal Society of Chemistry is the UK's professional body for chemical scientists, supporting the chemical sciences community through publishing, education, grants, and awards.

name: The CASS Foundation
description: Australian philanthropic foundation that supports research and career development in science, social sciences, and the arts, including travel awards for early career researchers attending international conferences.

name: University of Texas at Austin
description: Major public research university and flagship institution of the University of Texas System, located in Austin, Texas.
